titleStmt.title: Paris, Bibliothèque nationale de France, BnF Éthiopien d'Abbadie 102
Nagara Māryām, malkǝʾ-hymns and other texts

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

extent.note: Two unnumbered leaves at the beginning and one at the end appear to be a part of the European binding. One parchement leaf at the end of the volume has not been accounted for in the catalogues.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation in the upper right corner of the folia. The last parchment leaf, unnumbered, has been assigned the number .

collation.note: The quire structure cannot be ascertained on the basis of the available images.

binding.decoNote: European binding (according to A. d'Abbadie, the manuscript was originally in a damaged Ethiopian binding).
Pairs of sewing stations used irregularly.
According to A. d'Abbadie, the manuscript had a poorly preserved leather case.

history.origin: 18th/early 19th century. Unit 1 was copied in blue ink and extraordinary richly illuminated in the 18th century. At a somewhat later point Unit 2, contemporary or of a slightly later time, was introduced to Unit 1 (having been attached to ), possibly with the aim of extending the iconographic program with one important miniature ().
history.provenance: The manuscript was acquired by Antoine d'Abbadie sometime between 1840 and 1848.

msItem.title: Collection of salām-hymns (general record)
msItem.incipit: በእንተ፡ ዝንቱ፡ ኅብስት፡ ሥጋከ፡ ዘሥጋዌሁ፡ መስቀል፨ በእንተ፡ ደምከ፡ ክቡር፡ ቤዛ፡ ኀጣን፡ እምኃጕል፨

msItem.title: Nagara Māryām
msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ አብ፡ ንቀድም፡ በረድኤተ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ወንጽሕፍ፡ ዘንተ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ዘይሰመይ፡ በነገረ፡ ግብጻውያን፡ ንኡስ፡ ወንጌል፨ ወበይትዮጵያውያን፡ ነገረ፡ ማርያም፡

msItem.title: ʾO-rǝḫrǝḫta ḫǝllinā
msItem.incipit: ኦርኅርኅተ፡ ሕሊና፡ አፍቅሮተ፡ ሰብእ፡ ልማዳ፨ ሰአሊተ፡ ምሕረት፡ ይእቲ፡ ማርያም፡ ሐፁረ፡ መስቀል፡ ዘየዓውዳ፨

msItem.title: Several miracles of the Virgin Mary
msItem.incipit: ተአምሪሃ፡ ለእግዝእትነ፡ ቅድስት፡ ወድንግልት፡ ወሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ጊዮርጊስ ሐዲስ፡ መስተጋድል፡ ፈድፋደ፡ በከዊነ፡ ስምዕ፨

msItem.title: One Miracle of St George: St George appears to a pagan from Bǝbā who entered his church
msItem.incipit:
ተአምሪሁ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ ወብፁዕ፡ ማር፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ በረከተ፡
ተብህለ፡ በአሐቲ፡ ሀገር፡ እምአህጉረ፡ ግብጽ፡ ዘትሰመይ፡ ብባ፨ ወ
ሀሎ፡ ፩ብእሲ፡ ዘስሙ፡ ዮሐንስ፡ ዓቃቤ፡ ቤተ፡ ክርስቲያን፡

msItem.title: One Miracle of Archangel Michael: Miracle on the man from whom the evil spirit was cast out during Mass service
msItem.incipit: ተአምሪሁ፡ ለሊቀ፡ መላእክት፡ ሚካኤል፡ መልአከ፡ ሠርዌሆሙ፡ ለመላእክት፡ ወኮነ፡ ካዕበ፡ በአሐቲ፡ ዕለት፡ አመ፡ በዓሉ፡ ለሊቀ፡ መላእክት፡ ሚካኤል፡ ወኤጲስቆጶስ፡ አብ፡ እንዘ፡ ሀለዉ፡ ምስለ፡ ሕዘበ፡ ክርስቲያን፡ ውሳጤ፡ መርጡል፨

msItem.title: One Miracle of Archangel Gabriel
msItem.incipit:
ተአምሪሁ፡ ወመንክራቲሁ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ ገብርኤል፡
ወበውእቱ፡ ሰዓተ፡ መዓልት፡ እንተ፡ ጊዜ፡ ይበውኡ፡ ሥዕሎ፡ ለቅዱስ፡ ግብርኤል፡ ወሶቤሃ፡ ተገብረ፡ ተኣምር፡ ዓቢይ፡

msItem.title: Collection of salām-hymns (general record)
msItem.incipit: ሰላም፡ እብል፡ እሎንተ፡ ፫እደወ፨ ሞኡ፡ እሉ፡ አርዌ፡ ባሕር፡ ሌዋወ፨ አብርሃም፡ ይስሐቅ፡ ወያዕቆብ፡
msItem.note: Possibly a continution of or vice versa.

layout.ab: Pricking is visible on the images.
Ruling is partly visible on the images.

handNote.seg: Black, blue, red. According to A. d'Abbadie, the text on the first three leaves is written in black, the rest (the text on is probably meant) is written in blue ink, ʻle seul exemple venu à ma connaissance d'un livre éthiopien écrit en encre bleueʼ).
handNote.desc: Careful, slightly irregular; by a good scribe; described by A. d'Abbadie as ʻun beau guịlḥʼ.

decoNote.desc: St Stephan being stoned.
Nativity of Mary.
Presentation of Mary.
Miracle of Mary: Elizabeth, the blind daughter of Abraham and Gerā ʾAnǝśt of Badǝrmān.
Flight into Egypt.
How the Virgin Mary expelled the Devil from a man, who came out in the shape of boar.
How the Virgin Mary expelled a snake from the belly of a man.
How the Virgin Mary raised a man from the death.
How the Virgin Mary raised a man from the dead.
How the Virgin Mary healed sick people who hosted her.
How the Virgin Mary healed the man whose flesh was cut.
How the Virgin Mary expelled a snake from a man through his mouth.
How the Virgin Mary healed the lame, the mute and the deaf.
How the Virgin Mary raised from the dead a son of the country's chief.
How the Virgin Mary raised someone from the dead.
How the Virgin Mary scared the people by working miracles.
The commissioner Manbara Krǝstos praying in front of the Virgin Mary.
How the Virgin Mary healed some sick people.
How the Virgin Mary taught them the law.
How the Holy Spirit descended upon peoples.
How the Virgin Mary expelled a demon who went out in the shape of boar.
How the Virgin Mary resurrected the dead with her prayer.
How the Virgin Mary felt pity for an old man and made him young again.
How the Virgin Mary returned to the old man his children.
How the Archangels punished the sorcerer.
How the children of the old man returned to him.
How the Virgin Mary had mercy upon the old man and his children.
How the Virgin Mary raised many people from the dead.
How the people praised the Virgin Mary playing harp.
How the Virgin Mary resurrected the dead.
How the Virgin Mary encountred a dead body carried on the road and resurrected it.
How the Virgin Mary prayed over the dead body.
How the Virgin Mary raised someone from the dead.
How the Virgin Mary resurrected an old man.
Miracle of Mary: Unidentified miracle: How St John the Baptist baptized the people.
How the Apostles brought the people .
Miracle of Mary: Unidentified miracle: How the Virgin Mary appeared to Giyorgis in the shape of a dove.

Miracle of Mary: Bārok of Finǝqi whom his enemies could not kill until he had confessed himself to a priest: How the man called Bārok was beaten by robbers and saved by the Virgin Mary (cp. Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the Phoenician evil man called Bārok).

Miracle of Mary: The deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos who recited the prayer tafaśśǝḥi and was healed by Mary: How the Virgin Mary appeared to the deacon Athanasius of Rome (cp. Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome).
How St George of Lydda scared a governor.
How Archangel Michael healed a man in the time of the Gospels reading.
How Archangel Michael beat Satan.
How Archangel Gabriel healed someone possessed by a demon, the prone figure of the owner below.
How God made a covenant with ʾAbrǝhāmAbraham, Isaac and ያዕቆብ፡, the prone figure of the owner below.
Resurrection of Jesus.
Covenant of Mercy, the prone figure of the donor.
Covenant of Mercy, the prone figure of the donor.
Hagiographic Scene: Antony receiving monastic insignia from an angel.
Hagiographic Scene: Antony receiving monastic insignia from an angel.
Hagiographic Scene: Antony receiving monastic insignia from an angel.
Hagiographic Scene: Latṣun praying.
Equestrian Saint: St Victor riding.
Equestrian Saint: St Eusebius riding.
Hagiographic Scene: Yoḥannǝs of Wifāt.

item.desc: Shelfmark of the Library glued onto the inner side of the front board.
A two lines incipit formula written in black and read ink, in the main .
Stamp and shelfmark of the Library.
Stamp and shelfmark of the Library.
Monthly readings are indicated by the names of the months (starting from maskaram) written in the upper margin in .
Monthly readings are indicated by the names of the months (starting from maskaram) written in the upper margin in .

history.origin: 18th century. The name of the original owner was erased throughout and the name of Manbara Krǝstos has been written over erasures in a later hand.

incipit.hi: ሰላም፡ ለከ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ዘልዳ፡ መስተፅዕነ፡ ፈረስ፡ ፃዕዳ፡

extent.note: Unit 2 is composed of only one single leaf. It was glued upon of in which a rectangular opening was cut (to let the text on the verso-side be visible).

layout.note: The original size of the leaf is unknown as its margins were cut, probably comparable to the other leaves of the volume.

handNote.desc: Somewhat careless, irregular; by a mediocre scribe.

decoNote.desc: The Virgin and Child, the prone figure of the owner depicted beneath. The miniature in painted in a style similar to the miniatures in Unit 1, but most probably by a different artist, and to a somewhat later time.

history.origin: 18th or early 19th century. Unit 2 is represented by a part of one sole single leaf mounted on the leaf (possibly originally left blank) of , which bears the miniature on one side and the beginning of the text on another side.

bibl.title: Notice sur les manuscrits éthiopiens de la Collection d’Abbadie
Extrait du Journal Asiatique
Catalogue des manuscrits éthiopiens de la Collection Antoine d’Abbadie
Catalogue raisonné de manuscrits éthiopiens appartenant à Antoine d’Abbadie

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Consulted on account of the miniature in Unit 2
